coverage.note: Coverage skipped — focus this round was mutation-validating coder v1 false-green fixes. Both critical fixes pass mutation, the 3 minor-coverage tests assert behavior (Error.Key, raw body, missing-path in channel).

summary: Coder v1 follow-up (commit 6965c89e4) cleanly addresses both critical false-greens flagged in tester v1. (1) FileRead_Build_LiteralMissingFile_WritesBuildWarning now creates a MemoryChannel via Channels.CreateMemoryChannel('builder'), drains the stream, and asserts the missing-path string is in the written text. Mutation-tested: commented out the warning write in file/read.cs:76 — test failed with 'Expected to contain definitely-missing-stage4.csv ... but found ""'. Reverted clean. (2) RuntimeDoubleWrapTests invokes list.first/get/last + math.add and asserts result.Value is not itself a Data; a 5th sweep test pins the full 18-handler Data<object> set via reflection so a new Data<object> handler forces the author to add a runtime test or narrow T. Mutation-tested: replaced Ok(first.Value) with Ok(first) in list/first.cs — test caught it with the exact 'footgun fired' message. Reverted clean. The 3 minor-coverage fixes also land honestly: JsonStreamSerializer's 4 malformed-input tests assert Error.Key='JsonDeserializeError'; TextStreamSerializer's ThrowingStream forces real IOException and asserts Error.Key; http BodyDispatch_BrokenJsonContentType_FallsBackToString asserts the body is the literal malformed string. Suites green: 3136/3136 C# (+12 new) + 221/221 PLang. No new findings.
